creator: empty.japan
caption:
One thing I didn’t expect about living in Nagano is how long sakura season lasts here.
Because the prefecture is so long and mountainous, everything blooms at its own pace - in some areas the petals are already falling, in others it’s just starting, and in the higher mountains, it hasn’t even begun yet.
The coolest part? Some of these places are only 30 minutes apart.

And I’m waiting for one tree in particular - the little wild sakura on my land.
It’s still too cold up there, nothing has bloomed yet.
But I keep checking every time I visit… maybe in a week or two.

Location: Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma, Togurajuku Sakura Park

transcription.text: Woo, woo, woo, woo, woo, woo, woo.
